# (PART) Part II {-}

# Business evaluation {#business}

## Comprehensive financials^[Data are automatically retrieved from [Google Finance](https://www.google.com/finance) and [Yahoo Finance](https://finance.yahoo.com/).]

```{r}
confident_return = 0.05
target_return = 0.2
per_no_growth = 7

AAA_return = read_csv(str_c("https://fred.stlouisfed.org/graph/fredgraph.csv?id=", "DAAA", "&cosd=", monthago, "&coed=", today,
                            "&fq=Daily&fam=avg&vintage_date=", today, "&revision_date=", today, "&nd=", monthago), col_names = T) %>% 
  mutate(DAAA = as.numeric(DAAA)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(DAAA)) %>%
  pull(DAAA) %>%
  .[length(.)]
```

```{r}
drive_auth("freeustand@gmail.com", scopes = "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive")
gs4_auth(token = drive_token(), scopes = "https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive")

company = drive_get("Investment/Valuation") %>% read_sheet(sheet = "Company") %>%
  mutate(Yahoo = Ticker %>% 
           str_replace("^(.+):(.+)$", "\\2.\\1") %>% 
           str_replace_all(c("\\.KRX" = ".KS",
                             "\\.KOSDAQ" = ".KQ",
                             "\\.NYSE" = "",
                             "\\.NASDAQ" = "",
                             "\\.EPA" = ".PA",
                             "\\.TPE" = ".TW"))) %>%
  relocate(Yahoo, .after = Ticker)

plan(multisession)

yahoo_data = future_map_dfr(company$Yahoo, ~{
  ticker = .
    
  tryCatch({
    Summary = read_html(file.path("https://finance.yahoo.com/quote", ticker), header = F) %>% bind_rows
    Profile = read_html(file.path("https://finance.yahoo.com/quote" , ticker, "profile"), which = 1, header = T)
    Statistics = read_html(file.path("https://finance.yahoo.com/quote", ticker, "key-statistics"), header = F) %>% map_dfr(~select(., 1:2))
    Statistics$V1 %<>% str_replace(" [0-9]$", "")
    Revenue = read_html(file.path("https://finance.yahoo.com/quote", ticker, "analysis"), which = 2, header = F)
    Growth = read_html(file.path("https://finance.yahoo.com/quote", ticker, "analysis"), which = 6, header = T)
    
    tibble(Yahoo = ticker,
           Beta = Summary %>% filter(V1 == "Beta (5Y Monthly)") %>% pull(V2),
           Target = Summary %>% filter(V1 == "1y Target Est") %>% pull(V2),
           CEO = Profile %>% slice(1) %>% pull(Name),
           CEO_title = Profile %>% slice(1) %>% pull(Title),
           CEO_pay = Profile %>% slice(1) %>% pull(Pay),
           CEO_age = thisyear - (Profile %>% slice(1) %>% pull(`Year Born`) %>% as.numeric),
           Profit_margin = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Profit Margin") %>% pull(V2),
           ROA = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Return on Assets (ttm)") %>% pull(V2),
           ROE = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Return on Equity (ttm)") %>% pull(V2),
           Revenue_per_share = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Revenue Per Share (ttm)") %>% pull(V2),
           Quarterly_revenue_growth = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Quarterly Revenue Growth (yoy)") %>% pull(V2),
           Diluted_EPS = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Diluted EPS (ttm)") %>% pull(V2),
           Quarterly_earnings_growth = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Quarterly Earnings Growth (yoy)") %>% pull(V2),
           DER = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Total Debt/Equity (mrq)") %>% pull(V2),
           BVPS = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Book Value Per Share (mrq)") %>% pull(V2),
           Operating_cash_flow = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Operating Cash Flow (ttm)") %>% pull(V2),
           Levered_free_cash_flow = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Levered Free Cash Flow (ttm)") %>% pull(V2),
           Forward_annual_dividend_yield = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Forward Annual Dividend Yield") %>% pull(V2),
           Trailing_annual_dividend_yield = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Trailing Annual Dividend Yield") %>% pull(V2),
           Average_dividend_yield_5yrs = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "5 Year Average Dividend Yield") %>% pull(V2),
           Payout_ratio = Statistics %>% filter(V1 == "Payout Ratio") %>% pull(V2),
           Revenue_growth_estimate_current = Revenue %>% filter(V1 == "Sales Growth (year/est)") %>% pull(V4),
           Revenue_growth_estimate_next = Revenue %>% filter(V1 == "Sales Growth (year/est)") %>% pull(V5),
           Earnings_growth_past_5yrs = Growth %>% filter(`Growth Estimates` == "Past 5 Years (per annum)") %>% pull(ticker),
           Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs = Growth %>% filter(`Growth Estimates` == "Next 5 Years (per annum)") %>% pull(ticker))
  },
  error = function(cond) {
    message(str_c("Error: ", ticker))
    message(cond)
    return(NULL)
  })
})

plan("default")

company %<>% left_join(yahoo_data)

company %<>% 
  replace(. == "N/A", NA) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Beta, 
                  Target, 
                  Revenue_per_share,
                  Diluted_EPS, 
                  BVPS), 
                ~as.numeric(str_replace(., ",", "")))) %>%
  mutate(across(c(DER, 
                  Average_dividend_yield_5yrs), 
                ~as.numeric(.)/100)) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Profit_margin, 
                  ROA,
                  ROE, 
                  Quarterly_revenue_growth, 
                  Quarterly_earnings_growth, 
                  Forward_annual_dividend_yield, 
                  Trailing_annual_dividend_yield,
                  Payout_ratio, 
                  Revenue_growth_estimate_current, 
                  Revenue_growth_estimate_next, 
                  Earnings_growth_past_5yrs, 
                  Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs), 
                ~as.numeric(str_replace(., "%", ""))/100)) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Operating_cash_flow, Levered_free_cash_flow),
                ~case_when(str_detect(., "M$") ~ str_replace(., "M", "") %>% as.numeric %>% multiply_by(10e6),
                           str_detect(., "B$") ~ str_replace(., "B", "") %>% as.numeric %>% multiply_by(10e9),
                           str_detect(., "T$") ~ str_replace(., "T", "") %>% as.numeric %>% multiply_by(10e12))))

company %<>% 
  rowwise %>%
  mutate(PSR = Price / Revenue_per_share,
         PBR = Price / BVPS,
         Retention_ratio = 1 - Payout_ratio,
         Sustainable_growth_rate = ROE * Retention_ratio,
         Expected_EPS_growth_rate = Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs,
         Expected_payout_ratio_change_rate = 0,
         Expected_dividend_growth_rate = Expected_EPS_growth_rate * (1 + Expected_payout_ratio_change_rate),
         Expected_PE_in_10yrs = `Industry PER`,
         Estimated_price_in_10yrs = EPS * (1 + Expected_EPS_growth_rate)^10 * Expected_PE_in_10yrs,
         Reinvested_dividend_in_10yrs = sum(EPS * Payout_ratio * (1 + Expected_dividend_growth_rate)^(1:10) * (1 + confident_return)^(10 - 1:10)),
         Expected_price_in_10yrs = Estimated_price_in_10yrs + Reinvested_dividend_in_10yrs,
         DCF10 = Expected_price_in_10yrs / (1 + target_return)^10,
         Adjusted_Graham_formula = EPS * (per_no_growth + 100 * Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs) * 4.4 / AAA_return,
         `Price/Target` = Price/Target,
         PEG = PER/(100 * Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs),
         `Price/DCF10` = Price / DCF10,
         `Price/Graham` = Price / Adjusted_Graham_formula,
         `Relative_price` = exp(mean(log(c(`Price/Target`, PEG, `Price/DCF10`, `Price/Graham`) %>% sort %>% .[2:3])))) %>%
  rowwise %>%
  mutate(Decision = case_when(BVPS < 0 ~ "Negative equity",
                              DER > 2.5 ~ "High debt-to-equity ratio",
                              EPS <= 0 ~ "Zero or negative earnings",
                              Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs <= 0 ~ "Zero or negative growth",
                              ROE < 0.05 ~ "ROE < 5%",
                              Relative_price < 1 ~ "Buy",
                              sum(`Price/Target` < 1, PEG < 1, `Price/DCF10` < 1, `Price/Graham` < 1) > 1 ~ "Maybe buy",
                              T ~ "Wait")) %>%
  ungroup %>%
  relocate(Diluted_EPS, Revenue_per_share, BVPS, .after = EPS) %>%
  relocate(Revenue_growth_estimate_current, Revenue_growth_estimate_next, Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs,
           Operating_cash_flow, Levered_free_cash_flow,
           Profit_margin,
           DER, ROA, ROE, PER, `Industry PER`, PSR, PBR, .before = `Price/Target`) %>%
  relocate(Retention_ratio, .after = Payout_ratio)

company_plot = company

company %<>% 
  arrange(Industry, Name) %>%
  rename(`Debt-to-equity ratio` = DER,
         `5yrs average dividend yield` = Average_dividend_yield_5yrs,
         `Revenue growth estimate (current year)` = Revenue_growth_estimate_current,
         `Revenue growth estimate (next year)` = Revenue_growth_estimate_next,
         `Earnings growth (past 5yrs)` = Earnings_growth_past_5yrs,
         `Earnings growth estimate (next 5yrs)` = Earnings_growth_estimate_next_5yrs)

names(company) %<>% str_replace_all(., "_", " ")
```

```{r}
company %>% write_sheet(ss = drive_get("Investment/Valuation"), sheet = "Valuation")
```

```{r watchlist, fig.cap = "Comprehensive financial data of my watchlist"}
company_display = company %>% 
  select(-c(Ticker, Yahoo, Ticker3)) %>%
  mutate(Changepct = make_percent(Changepct, 1)) %>%
  mutate(across(c(`Expected payout ratio change rate`, 
                  `Payout ratio`, 
                  `Retention ratio`, 
                  `Profit margin`, 
                  `Debt-to-equity ratio`, 
                  ROA, 
                  ROE, 
                  contains(c("growth", "yield"))), 
                ~make_percent(., 100))) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Marketcap, 
                  `Operating cash flow`, 
                  `Levered free cash flow`), 
                make_readable)) %>%
  mutate(across(c(Price:Closeyest,
                  Low52:Target, 
                  `Expected PE in 10yrs`:`Adjusted Graham formula`,
                  PER:`Relative price`), 
                ~round(., 1) %>% format(scientific = F, big.mark = ",", drop0trailing = T) %>% str_trim)) %>%
  replace(. == "NA", NA)

my_datatable(company_display, paging = T, pageLength = 5, scrollY = NULL, caption = NULL)
```

Adjusted Graham formula: as defined [here](https://www.oldschoolvalue.com/stock-valuation/benjamin-graham-formula/)\
BVPS: book value per share\
DCF10: discounted cash flow in 10 years as defined [here](https://www.investopedia.com/terms/d/dcf.asp)\
EPS: earnings per share\
High52: 52-week high price\
Industry PER: data from [here](http://pages.stern.nyu.edu/~adamodar/New_Home_Page/datafile/pedata.html)\
Low52: 52-week low price\
PBR: price/book value ratio\
PEG: price/earnings-to-growth ratio\
PER: price/earnings ratio\
PSR: price/sales ratio\
ROA: return on asset\
ROE: return on equity\
Sustainable growth rate: ROE $\times$ retention ratio

## Key financials

```{r}
company_plot %<>% 
  mutate(`Earnings-to-price` = 1/PER,
         `Book-to-price` = 1/PBR,
         `Sales-to-price` = 1/PSR) %>%
  arrange(desc(Industry), !is.na(`Earnings-to-price`), `Earnings-to-price`, !is.na(`Sales-to-price`), `Sales-to-price`, !is.na(`Book-to-price`), `Book-to-price`) %>%
  mutate(Name = factor(Name, levels = Name))

f1 = company_plot %>%
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = Name, y = 0)) + 
  geom_col(mapping = aes(fill = Industry), color = "transparent") +
  scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0, 0), limits = c(0, 0)) +
  scale_fill_manual(limits = unique(company_plot$Industry), 
                    values = qual_col_vector[length(unique(company$Industry)):1], 
                    guide = guide_legend(title = NULL, ncol = 3, reverse = T, override.aes = list(color = "black"))) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme(axis.ticks = element_blank(), axis.text.x = element_blank(),
        legend.position = "bottom",
        panel.background = element_rect(fill = "transparent"), 
        plot.margin = unit(c(5.5, 0, 5.5, 5.5), "points"))

financial_plot = function(financials, variable, title = NULL, labels = NULL) {
  if (is.null(title)) title = enquo(variable)
  if (is.null(labels)) labels = waiver()
  
  variable = enquo(variable)
  
  financials %>%
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = Name, y = !!variable)) + 
    geom_col(mapping = aes(fill = Industry), color = "black", width = 0.7) +
    scale_y_continuous(expand = c(0.05, 0), breaks = pretty_breaks(3), labels = labels) +
    scale_fill_manual(limits = unique(financials$Industry), values = qual_col_vector[length(unique(financials$Industry)):1], guide = F) +
    coord_flip() +
    labs(x = NULL, y = title) +
    theme(axis.ticks.y = element_blank(), axis.ticks.length.y = unit(0, "npc"), axis.text.y = element_blank(), plot.margin = unit(c(5.5, 5.5, 5.5, 0), "points"))
}

f2 = financial_plot(company_plot, `Earnings-to-price`, "Earnings/Price")
f3 = financial_plot(company_plot, `Sales-to-price`, "Sales/Price")
f4 = financial_plot(company_plot, `Book-to-price`, "Book/Price")
f5 = financial_plot(company_plot, ROE, "Return on equity")
f6 = financial_plot(company_plot, ROA, "Return on asset")
f7 = financial_plot(company_plot, DER, "Debt/Equity")
f8 = financial_plot(company_plot, Quarterly_earnings_growth, "Quarterly earnings growth (yoy)", percent)
f9 = financial_plot(company_plot, Quarterly_revenue_growth, "Quarterly revenue growth (yoy)", percent)

n_industry = company_plot$Industry %>% unique %>% length

legend_ratio = (nrow(company_plot) + 2) / (ceiling(n_industry/3) + 1)
name_width = 1.55 * max(stri_width(company_plot$Name)) / stri_width("Korea Shipbuilding &Offshore Engn Co Ltd")
plot_width = 4.55 - name_width
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = (nrow(company_plot) + 2) * 3/4 + (ceiling(n_industry/3) + 1)/4, fig.cap = "Key financials of my watchlist"}
ggarrange(ggarrange(f1 + guides(fill = F), f2, f3, f4, nrow = 1, ncol = 4, align = "h", widths = c(name_width, rep(plot_width/3, 3))), 
          ggarrange(f1 + guides(fill = F), f5, f6, f7, nrow = 1, ncol = 4, align = "h", widths = c(name_width, rep(plot_width/3, 3))),
          ggarrange(f1 + guides(fill = F), f8, f9, nrow = 1, ncol = 3, align = "h", widths = c(name_width, rep(plot_width/2, 2))),
          get_legend(f1), 
          nrow = 4, ncol = 1, 
          heights = c(legend_ratio, legend_ratio, legend_ratio, 1))
```

## Investment decision^[The decision is made by analyzing only numerical data, which do not represent the value of a business as a whole. For example, the value of an early-stage company with zero or negative earnings cannot be determined based on numbers, but rather should be assessed based on its mission, technology, management, and culture, as well as the state of the industry overall.]

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = (nrow(company) + 6)/4, fig.cap = "Investment decision made by a fundamental analysis-based algorithm for the listed companies"}
company_decision = company %>% 
  mutate(Decision = factor(Decision, levels = c("Buy",
                                                "Maybe buy",
                                                "Wait", 
                                                "ROE < 5%",
                                                "Zero or negative earnings", 
                                                "Zero or negative growth", 
                                                "High debt-to-equity ratio", 
                                                "Negative equity"))) %>%
  ggplot(mapping = aes(x = Name, y = Decision)) +
  geom_point(mapping = aes(fill = Decision), shape = 21, size = 3, stroke = 1) +
  scale_x_discrete(limits = sort(company$Name, decreasing = T)) +
  scale_fill_brewer(type = "qual", palette = 1) +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(x = NULL) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1), legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))

company_decision
```

```{r}
nona = function(x) ifelse(sum(!is.na(x)) > 0, x[!is.na(x)], NA)
yahoo_to_name = function(Yahoo) company$Name[match(Yahoo, company$Yahoo)]

plan(multisession)

company_close = company$Yahoo %>% future_map(~{
  read_html(file.path("https://finance.yahoo.com/quote", .x, "history"), which = 1, header = T) %>% select(Date, `Close*`)
})

plan("default")

company_close %<>% 
  setNames(company$Yahoo) %>% 
  imap_dfr(~mutate(.x, Yahoo = .y)) %>% 
  rename(Close = "Close*") %>% 
  mutate(Event = Close %>% 
           str_extract("^.+[a-zA-Z].+$") %>% 
           str_replace("Stock Split", " stock split") %>% 
           str_replace("Dividend", " dividend") %>%
           str_replace(",", ""))

company_close$Date %<>%
  str_match("([^ ,]+) ([^ ,]+), ([^ ,]+)") %>% 
  .[, -1] %>% 
  as_tibble %>% 
  setNames(c("m", "d", "y")) %>% 
  mutate(m = match(m, month.abb), date = str_c(y, m, d, sep = "-")) %>% 
  pull(date) %>% 
  as.Date

company_close %<>%
  group_by(Date, Yahoo) %>%
  summarise(Close = nona(as.numeric(str_replace(Close, ",", ""))), Event = nona(Event)) %>%
  ungroup %>%
  filter(!is.na(Close)) %>%
  arrange(Yahoo, Date) %>%
  left_join(company %>% select(Yahoo, Name, Industry))

company_with_sufficient_data = company_close %>% count(Name) %>% filter(n > 10) %>% pull(Name)

company_close %<>% 
  filter(Name %in% company_with_sufficient_data) %>%
  mutate(Name = str_replace_all(Name, c("Taiwan Semiconductor Mfg. Co. Ltd." = "TSMC")))

base_date = company_close %>% count(Date) %>% filter(n == length(company_with_sufficient_data)) %>% pull(Date) %>% min

get_legend_nrow = function(close, industry) {
  max(round((close %>% filter(Industry == industry) %>% pull(Name) %>% unique %>% length)/3), 1)
}

get_legend_nrow = Vectorize(get_legend_nrow, "industry")

close_trend = function(close, industry) {
  tmp = close %>% 
    filter(Industry == industry) %>%
    group_by(Name) %>%
    mutate(Close = Close / Close[Date == base_date] * 100) %>%
    ungroup
  
  ncompany = length(unique(tmp$Name))
  legend_nrow = max(round(ncompany/3), 1)
  
  g = tmp %>%
    ggplot(mapping = aes(x = Date, y = Close)) +
    geom_hline(yintercept = 100, linetype = "dashed") +
    geom_line(mapping = aes(color = Name)) +
    scale_x_date(expand = c(0, 0)) +
    scale_color_manual(values = qual_col_vector[-6][1:ncompany], 
                       guide = guide_legend(title = NULL, nrow = legend_nrow)) +
    labs(x = "Recent 6 months", y = "Normalized closing price") +
    theme(legend.position = "bottom", legend.key = element_rect(fill = "transparent"))
  
  ggarrange(g + guides(color = F), ggdraw(get_legend(g)), nrow = 2, ncol = 1, heights = c(8, legend_nrow))
}

interesting_sectors = company_close %>% select(Name, Industry) %>% unique %>% count(Industry) %>% arrange(desc(n), Industry) %>% pull(Industry)

fig_height = (8 + get_legend_nrow(company_close, interesting_sectors))/2.5
```

## Stock price trend^[Only top 15 industry sectors are shown. All prices are normalized to 100 as of `r base_date`.]

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[1], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[1]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[1])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[2], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[2]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[2])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[3], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[3]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[3])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[4], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[4]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[4])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[5], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[5]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[5])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[6], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[6]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[6])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[7], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[7]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[7])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[8], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[8]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[8])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[9], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[9]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[9])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[10], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[10]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[10])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[11], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[11]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[11])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[12], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[12]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[12])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[13], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[13]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[13])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[14], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[14]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[14])
```

```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = fig_height[15], fig.cap = interesting_sectors[15]}
close_trend(company_close, interesting_sectors[15])
```

## Stock price correlation^[Industry sector classification is based on data from [here](http://pages.stern.nyu.edu/~adamodar/New_Home_Page/datafile/pedata.html).]

```{r, include = F}
company_event = company_close %>% filter(!is.na(Event)) %>% select(Date, Yahoo, Event)

company_close_wide = company_close %>% select(-Event) %>% pivot_wider(id_cols = Date, names_from = Yahoo, values_from = Close)

company_cor = company_close_wide %>% column_to_rownames("Date") %>% cor(use = "pairwise.complete.obs")

colnames(company_cor) %<>% yahoo_to_name
rownames(company_cor) %<>% yahoo_to_name

company_annotation = company %>% 
  select(Name, `Broad group`, Country, Industry) %>% 
  column_to_rownames("Name") %>% 
  .[rownames(company_cor), ]

n_industry = company_annotation$Industry %>% unique %>% length

broad_group_color = qual_col_vector[1:length(unique(company_annotation$`Broad group`))] %>% setNames(sort(unique(company_annotation$`Broad group`)))
country_color = qual_col_vector[1:length(unique(company_annotation$Country))] %>% setNames(sort(unique(company_annotation$Country)))
industry_color = qual_col_vector[1:length(unique(company_annotation$Industry))] %>% setNames(sort(unique(company_annotation$Industry)))

column_annotation = HeatmapAnnotation(df = company_annotation,
                                      col = list(Industry = industry_color,
                                                 Country = country_color,
                                                 `Broad group` = broad_group_color),
                                      annotation_name_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8),
                                      annotation_legend_param = list(`Broad group` = list(grid_width = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                                                          grid_height = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                                                          title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8, fontface = "bold"),
                                                                                          labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 7)),
                                                                     Country = list(grid_width = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                                                    grid_height = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                                                    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8, fontface = "bold"),
                                                                                    labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 7)),
                                                                     Industry = list(ncol = 2, 
                                                                                     grid_width = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                                                     grid_height = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                                                     title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8, fontface = "bold"),
                                                                                     labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 7))))

h = company_cor %>% Heatmap(name = "Pearson's r",
                        width = unit(5, "inch"), height = unit(5, "inch"),
                        show_column_names = F, show_column_dend = T,
                        show_row_names = T, show_row_dend = F,
                        row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6),
                        heatmap_legend_param = list(legend_height = unit(ceiling(n_industry/2) * 4, "mm"),
                                                    grid_width = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8, fontface = "bold"),
                                                    labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 7)),
                        top_annotation = column_annotation) %>% 
  draw(merge_legend = T, heatmap_legend_side = "bottom")

h_height = h@ht_list_param$height %>% as.numeric %>% conv_unit("mm", "inch")
```


```{r, fig.width = 7, fig.height = h_height + 0.2, fig.cap = "Pairwise correlation between closing prices of my watchlist over the last 6 months"}
company_cor %>% Heatmap(name = "Pearson's r",
                        width = unit(5, "inch"), height = unit(5, "inch"),
                        show_column_names = F, show_column_dend = T,
                        show_row_names = T, show_row_dend = F,
                        row_names_gp = gpar(fontsize = 6),
                        heatmap_legend_param = list(legend_height = unit(ceiling(n_industry/2) * 4, "mm"),
                                                    grid_width = unit(4, "mm"),
                                                    title_gp = gpar(fontsize = 8, fontface = "bold"),
                                                    labels_gp = gpar(fontsize = 7)),
                        top_annotation = column_annotation) %>% 
  draw(merge_legend = T, heatmap_legend_side = "bottom")
```

## Small-cap companies

These are a list of small-cap companies which interest me but need more research. For comprehensive financial data, see Figure \@ref(fig:watchlist).

### Intellia Therapeutics^[Data are from [Wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Main_Page).]

[![](images/Intellia_Therapeutics.png){width=240px}](https://www.intelliatx.com/)

```{r}
smallcap = "Intellia_Therapeutics"

read_html(str_c("https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/", smallcap), which = 1, header = T, trim = T) %>% 
  mutate(V2 = str_replace_all(V2, c("\\[[0-9]+\\]$" = "", "([A-Za-z])\\(" = "\\1 ("))) %>%
  column_to_rownames("V1") %>% 
  t %>% 
  as_tibble %>%
  mutate(`Traded as` = str_replace_all(`Traded as`, "(.)(NASDAQ|Russell)", "\\1<br>\\2"),
         `Key people` = str_replace_all(`Key people`, "\\)([A-Z])", ")<br>\\1")) %>%
  t %>% 
  as_tibble(.name_repair = "minimal", rownames = "Var") %>%
  kbl(escape = F, col.names = NULL, caption = str_c("Profile of ", str_replace(smallcap, "_", " "))) %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover", "condensed", "responsive"),
                full_width = F, position = "left")
```

### SI-Bone^[Wiki page is not available. The official website is https://si-bone.com/.]

[![](images/SI_Bone.png){width=240px}](https://si-bone.com/)

### CymaBay Therapeutics^[Wiki page is not available. The official website is https://www.cymabay.com/.]

[![](images/CymaBay_Therapeutics.png){width=240px}](https://www.cymabay.com/)
